w:r.w:t: (keeps public pages fast, while sensitive files stay private):
For org logos + listing thumbnails (safe to be public)
For listing original images, PDFs/spec sheets, and message attachments
) in DB, never store signed URLs.
Signed URLs shouldn’t be cached publicly. Use:
In UI, cache in memory (React state) for the page session
user a member of an org?
-- Helper: is member AND role is owner/admin?
Sellers can read their own drafts etc.
policy "listing spec write by seller admins"
policy "listing spec update by seller admins"
policy "listing media write by seller admins"
policy "listing media delete by seller admins"
create policy "inquiry read buyer or seller"
-- RFQ: buyer org can see/manage; seller org can see only if targeted
target read buyer or seller target"
-- Quotes: seller can create/manage their quote; buyer can read all quotes for their RFQ
policy "quote read buyer or owning seller"
policy "quote line items read via quote"
policy "quote line items write by owning seller admins"
Messaging (threads visible to buyer/seller org members
create policy "thread read buyer or seller"
policy "thread create buyer or seller members"
policy "message send only if sender org is participant"
Events (write: members only; read: org members)
4.1 Helper functions to parse object paths
-- Can current user read listing private files?
-- Can current user write listing private files? (
-- Can read message attachments for a thread?
-- WRITE: message attachments (allow if thread participant; you can tighten further by requiring sender org via DB flow)
in UI; no RLS required for reads. Restrict writes:
policy "public-assets write org assets by org admins"
If you want Postgres RLS to work with
first, then run queries. That keeps the same connection for the transaction.
5.2 Use it in a Next.js Server Action / Route
and never call it from the browser-facing request path unless you do explicit authorization checks.
You can do this via a
Edge Function or Next.js server route + sharp
On message render, sign URLs server-side
